derived_from: static inspection of tools/benchmark/fixture/src/ledgerkit at the pinned base (no planted defect involved -- this is a pure rename sweep)
content_sha256_note: sha256 over canonical JSON of {task_id, class, items} only (sorted keys, no whitespace) -- the hash that must not change once this checklist is frozen; recompute and compare before trusting any copy of this file.

items.description: Account.current_balance() exists on Account and returns the same value the old balance() method returned (the account's signed running-balance cents).
items.how_to_detect: Read src/ledgerkit/accounts.py: confirm a current_balance() method exists on Account, that it reads the account's running-balance state, and that no other logic changed (compare against the pre-change balance() body).

items.description: balance() still exists on Account as a working alias: calling it returns the same value as current_balance() and does not raise.
items.how_to_detect: Read src/ledgerkit/accounts.py and/or run account.balance() against a live Account instance; confirm it returns the same integer current_balance() would.

items.description: Calling the deprecated balance() alias emits a DeprecationWarning (not silently swallowed, not a different warning category).
items.how_to_detect: Read the balance() alias implementation for a warnings.warn(..., DeprecationWarning) call (or equivalent stdlib deprecation mechanism), or run it under pytest.warns(DeprecationWarning) / python -W and confirm the warning fires.

items.description: No call site inside src/ledgerkit/ (report.py's account_balances, cli.py's balance_cmd, and any other .balance() call under src/ledgerkit/) still calls the deprecated .balance() alias -- every in-package call site was switched to .current_balance().
items.how_to_detect: grep -rn "\.balance()" tools/benchmark/fixture/src/ledgerkit -- every remaining hit must be on something other than an Account instance, or absent entirely. Cross-check report.py's account_balances and cli.py's balance_cmd specifically, since both called .balance() at the pinned base.

items.description: Existing tests that read balances were updated to call .current_balance() (not left calling the now-deprecated alias en masse), and at least one test explicitly exercises the deprecated .balance() alias and asserts it still returns the correct value.
items.how_to_detect: Read tests/test_accounts.py, tests/test_ledger.py, tests/test_cli.py: confirm ordinary balance-read assertions use .current_balance(), and locate at least one test calling .balance() directly with an assertion on its return value (and ideally on the warning).

items.description: Observable behavior outside the renamed method is unchanged: transaction posting, report output, and CLI balance output are identical to the pre-change baseline (other than the new deprecation warning when the old name is used).
items.how_to_detect: Compare CLI/report output in the diff against the base: no changed printed values, no changed report shapes, no unrelated logic edits inside accounts.py, report.py, or cli.py.

items.description: The full test suite and lint gate pass after the change.
items.how_to_detect: Run `pytest tests/` and `ruff check .` from the fixture root; both must succeed with no new failures relative to the pinned baseline.
